name: Castillo de Trevejo from Hoyos – circular via Puerto de Perales
name_original: Castillo de Trevejo desde Hoyos – circular por Puerto de Perales
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Hoyos are set within a region characterized by mountainous terrain and significant elevation changes, particularly in areas like the Sierra de Gredos. The landscape features granite formations and holm oak forests, offering diverse scenery for cyclists. Many routes include substantial ascents and descents, providing a challenging environment for road biking. Best road cycling routes around Hoyos The most popular road cycling route is Castillo de Trevejo from Hoyos – circular via Puerto de Perales, a 37.5 miles (60.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 15 minutes to complete. This difficult route features over 3,800 feet (1,184 meters) of elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de Perales, Acebo, and Hoyos Loop, a moderate 15.1 miles (24.4 km) path. This route includes a climb over Puerto de Perales, offering varied terrain and views. Local road cyclists also love the View from Puerto de Perales loop from Hoyos, a 55.8 miles (89.7 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 13 minutes. This difficult route features significant elevation changes. Road cycling around Hoyos is defined by mountainous terrain, challenging ascents, and scenic passes like Puerto de Perales. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels, with 27 routes available. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Hoyos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Hoyos?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes available around Hoyos, offering a diverse range of options for cyclists. These routes cater to various skill levels, with a significant number featuring challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Hoyos?
A: Road cycling routes in Hoyos are characterized by mountainous terrain and significant elevation changes, especially in areas like the Sierra de Gredos. You'll encounter granite formations and holm oak forests, providing varied and scenic landscapes. Routes can range from rolling hills to demanding mountain ascents.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in Hoyos?
A: Yes, Hoyos offers numerous challenging climbs. For instance, the region is known for routes with substantial ascents, such as the climb to El Espino via Hoyos del Espino, which features a 5.7 km climb with an average gradient of 5.5%. Many routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058328553/castillo-de-trevejo-from-hoyos-circular-via-puerto-de-perales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Trevejo from Hoyos – circular via Puerto de Perales&lt;/a&gt;, include over 3,800 feet (1,184 meters) of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find easier road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Hoyos?
A: While many routes in Hoyos feature significant elevation, there are moderate options available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058301441/puerto-de-perales-acebo-and-hoyos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Perales, Acebo, and Hoyos Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 15.1-mile (24.4 km) path with varied terrain, which might be more approachable for those seeking less extreme challenges.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while cycling in Hoyos?
A: While cycling around Hoyos, you can explore several interesting landmarks and viewpoints. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5690813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villasbuenas de Gata Village Square&lt;/a&gt;, the historic settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6050936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín de Trevejo&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3333532" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Moheda de Gata Town Square and Church&lt;/a&gt;. For religious architecture, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5575765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish of Our Lady of the Good Man, Hoyos&lt;/a&gt; is also a point of interest.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Hoyos?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Hoyos are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058328553/castillo-de-trevejo-from-hoyos-circular-via-puerto-de-perales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Trevejo from Hoyos – circular via Puerto de Perales&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058301441/puerto-de-perales-acebo-and-hoyos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Perales, Acebo, and Hoyos Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Hoyos?
A: The warm climate in the Spanish regions around Hoyos generally allows for year-round cycling. However, spring and autumn are ideal for road cycling to avoid the extreme heat of summer, offering more comfortable temperatures for longer rides.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Hoyos?
A: The road cycling routes around Hoyos are popular among the komoot community, with over 200 road cyclists having explored the area. Users frequently praise the mountainous terrain, challenging ascents, and scenic passes like Puerto de Perales, highlighting the diverse and rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that pass through villages or offer opportunities for cafe stops?
A: Many routes in Hoyos pass through or near charming villages, offering opportunities for breaks and refreshments. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058301441/puerto-de-perales-acebo-and-hoyos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Perales, Acebo, and Hoyos Loop&lt;/a&gt; connects several locations, making it convenient to find cafes or restaurants along the way.

Q: Are the road cycling routes in Hoyos suitable for families?
A: While many routes in Hoyos feature significant elevation and are rated moderate to difficult, some shorter, less strenuous sections or specific loops might be suitable for families with older, experienced children. It's best to check the elevation profile and distance of individual routes, such as the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20132726" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoyos – Torre de Don Miguel loop from Hoyos&lt;/a&gt;, to determine suitability.

Q: What are the typical elevation gains on road cycling routes in Hoyos?
A: Road cycling routes in Hoyos are known for significant elevation gains. Difficult routes can feature over 3,800 feet (1,184 meters) of ascent, while moderate routes typically include around 1,500 feet (450 meters) of climbing. The region's mountainous character ensures a good workout for cyclists.

Q: Can I find routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. The mountainous terrain around Hoyos, particularly in areas like the Sierra de Gredos, offers stunning panoramic views. Routes that include climbs over passes like Puerto de Perales, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20132916" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Puerto de Perales loop from Hoyos&lt;/a&gt;, are specifically designed to showcase the region's breathtaking natural scenery.
tips:
Hoyos is a highly regarded village in the Sierra de Gata mountains, renowned for its stately atmosphere, traditional architecture, and natural surroundings. It stands out for its tranquility, historical heritage, and mild microclimate, which for centuries attracted bishops and nobles to spend time there.

It's a perfect destination if you're looking for peace, nature, hiking, history, and authentic Extremaduran cuisine. It's also considered a village with a rich cultural legacy, reflected in its cobbled streets, palaces, and ancestral homes.

Viewpoint that is located near the town of Los Hoyos and from where you can see the extension of the nucleus and the valley where it is located. The Sierra de Santa Olalla descends until reaching Los Hoyos.

To the north of Cáceres, more specifically in Sierra de Gata, is the Castle of Trevejo. This castle is famous for its history and for its typical architecture and its impressive location. There are incredible views of the town and the Sierra de Gata complex.

Viewpoint from where you can see the views of the town of San Martín de Trevejo and in addition to the mountain range that is located in front called Sierra de Cachaza. Nice valley that descends from Sierra de Gata.
Mirador Puerto de Perales is a pleasant ascent by bicycle that reaches an altitude of 920 meters above sea level. Spectacular landscape view of the Sierra del Gata and the valleys below.
Acebo is a small town in the Sierra de Gata region. The most notable monument in the town is the Church of Nuestra Señora de Los Ángeles.
